description: In an apartment, Chandler enters through the front door holding a folded umbrella and a newspaper. He walks to the kitchen table, sets them down, and eats a piece of food from a plate.
Chandler walks to the refrigerator and opens it. Elsewhere, Rachel steps out of a doorway wearing a towel around her waist, drying her hair with a smaller towel, her upper body bare.
Rachel walks into the living room and spots Chandler. Both scream loudly. Rachel quickly pulls a large crocheted blanket around her chest and shoulders.
Chandler drops an object on the floor, raising his hands and repeatedly shouting apologies over Rachel's loud shouts about him barging in.
Rachel continues to speak quickly, adjusting the blanket around herself. Chandler stands by the refrigerator.
Chandler says, "It. Just say one thing."
Rachel asks, "What? What?" Chandler gestures and says, "That's a relatively open weave and I can still see your"
Rachel looks down at the blanket she is clutching.
Chandler continues, "nippular area."
Rachel gasps, pulls the blanket tighter, and quickly walks away into a hallway.
Chandler watches her leave, then throws his hands up and tilts his head back with his mouth open.
The scene changes to the exterior of a coffee shop at night, with a sign reading "CENTRAL PERK." Inside, Monica, Phoebe, Rachel, and Roger are seated around a small table. Monica touches Roger's arm.
Monica says, "Tell them the story about your patient who thinks things are like other things."
Monica looks at the others and says, "You know?"
Roger gestures with his free hand.
Roger, holding a mug, says, "So like, when the phone rings and she takes a shower"
Rachel smiles and nods.
Roger continues, "that's pretty much it."
The group laughs, and Monica smiles at Roger.
Monica says, "But you tell it really well, sweetie." Roger says, "Thanks," leaning his head toward hers.
Monica smiles at Roger.
Monica says, "Okay? Now go away so we can talk about you."
Roger nods.
Roger stands up and says, "Okay. I'll miss you."
Roger walks away.
Phoebe leans in and asks, "Isn't he great?" Rachel says, "He's so cute."
Rachel adds, "And he seems to like you so much." Monica smiles and says, "I know. So sweet. So"
Monica continues, "complicated. And for a shrink, he's not too shrinky."
Phoebe shifts in her seat.
Phoebe asks, "So you think you'll do it on his couch?" Monica replies, "Oh, I don't know. I think that's a little weird."
Phoebe looks closely at Monica.
Phoebe asks, "Vinyl?"
Monica gestures while smiling. Rachel stands up and steps away from the table.

Rachel, wearing an apron, walks over to Chandler and Ross, who are sitting on a couch. She asks, "Okay, you guys want anything else?" Chandler raises his hand and says, "Oh, yes. Could I have one of those?"
Rachel says, "No, I'm sorry, we're all out of those. Anybody else?" Ross says, "Hey,"
Ross looks at Chandler.
Ross asks, "did I miss something?" Chandler says, "No, she's still upset because I saw her"
Rachel stands with her hands on her hips, staring at Chandler.
Ross asks Chandler, "what were you doing seeing her boobies?" Chandler says, "It was an accident."
Chandler gestures with his hands, adding, "It's not like I was across the street with a telescope and a box of donuts."
Rachel maintains her stance with one hand on her hip.
Rachel says, "Okay, can we change the subject, please?" Phoebe stands and approaches, saying, "Yeah."
Phoebe points at Rachel's chest and says, "You know, these are not her boobies. These are her breasts."
Rachel closes her eyes and exhales.
Rachel says, "Okay, Phoebes, I was hoping of more of a"
Monica looks toward the group.
Monica says, "know. I always like bazumbas."
Monica moves her hands.
Monica gestures outward with both hands and says, "Kind of give them a Latin spin."
Rachel presses her lips together.
Rachel raises her hands and says, "Can we drop this already?"
Chandler points toward Rachel while remaining seated on the couch.
Chandler says, "I don't know why you're so embarrassed. They were very nice boobies."
Rachel walks to an armchair and sits down.
Rachel gestures and says, "Nice? They were nice?"
Rachel continues, "I mean, that's it? I mean, mittens are nice."
Chandler looks at Rachel.
Chandler says, "Okay, rock hard place."
Chandler points to himself and says, "Me."
Roger sits down next to Phoebe behind the couch, holding a pastry. He laughs loudly, pointing at Chandler, as Phoebe touches his hair.
Phoebe says, "You're."
Phoebe smiles at Roger.
Phoebe continues, "You're so funny. He's really funny." Roger's laughter continues.
Roger's laughter stops abruptly as he looks at Chandler.
Roger says, "I wouldn't want to be there when the laughter stops."
Ross raises his hand.
Ross says, "Whoa, whoa. Back up there, sparky."
Chandler stares at Roger.
Chandler asks, "What did you mean by that?"
Roger maintains his gaze on Chandler.
Roger says, "Just seems as though that maybe you have intimacy issues."
Roger continues, "That you use your humor as a way of keeping people at a distance."
Roger smiles slightly at Chandler.
Inside a coffee shop, Roger sits on a couch holding a piece of food, gesturing with his other hand. Chandler sits next to him.
Roger gestures with his hand and says, "I mean, hey, I just met you."
Chandler looks at Roger as Roger says, "I don't know you from Adam."
Roger lowers his hand, still holding the food.
Roger asks, "Only child. Right?" while leaning his head back slightly. Chandler continues looking at him.
Chandler swallows visibly.
Roger gestures with the food and says, "Parents divorced before you hit puberty."
Chandler looks downward briefly. Ross, sitting nearby with a notebook, looks toward Roger.
Chandler asks, "How did you know that?" Roger takes a bite of his food and replies, "It's textbook."
Joey walks into the coffee shop wearing a long coat, followed by an older man, Joey Tribbiani Sr.
Joey raises a hand and says, "Hey, you guys."
The group exchanges greetings. Joey Sr. points at the group. Phoebe stands and places her hands on the back of the couch near Roger.
A woman asks, "Hey. How long are you in the city?" Joey Sr. gestures and replies, "Just for a couple of days. I got a job midtown."
Joey Sr. continues, "I figure I'm better off staying with the kid than hauling my ass back and forth on a ferry."
Joey Sr. points toward Roger and says, "I don't know this one." Phoebe touches Roger's shoulder and says, "Oh, this is my friend Roger."
Joey Sr. shakes Roger's hand and says, "Hey, good to meet you, Roger." Roger nods and says, "You too, sir." Joey Sr. begins, "What happened to the"
Joey Sr. points a finger and finishes, "puppet guy?"
Joey steps forward and places a hand on Joey Sr.'s chest, pushing him gently.
Joey says, "Excuse me," leading his dad away. Joey Sr. leans over the couch toward Ross and asks, "So, Ross, how's the wife?"
Ross lowers his head. Chandler and Monica look downward.
Joey Sr. gestures with both hands and says, "Oh, for two," as Joey pulls him away.
Joey and his dad walk toward the counter. Ross turns his head toward Chandler.
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "Chandler, quick, say something funny."
Chandler sits motionless, his hands clasped, keeping his gaze straight ahead.
The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night.
Inside an apartment, Joey Sr. sits in a chair holding a telephone receiver. He says, "Gotta go."
Joey Sr. smiles and says, "Miss you too. I love you, but it's getting real late now," holding his free hand up.
Joey walks into the room holding a cordless phone to his ear. He says, "Hey, Ma. Listen, I made the appointment with Dr."
Joey stops walking and says, "Bezita and"
Joey stops talking, lowering the phone slightly as his eyes widen, looking at his dad.
Joey speaks into his phone, "excuse me."
Joey holds the phone against his chest, keeping his gaze fixed on his dad without smiling.
Joey asks, "Did you know this isn't Ma?"
Joey Sr. pulls the phone away from his ear, pressing a finger to his lips.
Joey Sr. coughs softly into his hand while holding the phone.
Now in a kitchen, Joey chops mushrooms on a cutting board. Joey Sr. stands nearby.
Joey Sr. points a finger and says, "She's a pet mortician."
Joey stops chopping, glances up at his dad, then resumes chopping.
Joey replies, "Sure."
Joey Sr. keeps his gaze on Joey.
Joey asks, "So how long you been?"
Joey continues chopping while his dad looks at him.
Joey Sr. gestures and asks, "Remember when you were a little kid I used to take you to the Navy yard and show you the big ships?"
Joey asks, "Since then" and Joey Sr. says, "no, it's only been"
Joey Sr. continues, "six years. I just wanted to put a nice memory in your head so you'd know that I wasn't always such a terrible guy."
Joey Sr. leans forward on the counter, watching Joey chop.
Joey Sr. asks, "What are you doing?" Joey replies, "Chopping garlic."
Joey Sr. says, "You're not going to crush it."
Joey points his knife toward his dad and says, "Hey, you're having an affair, I'm chopping the garlic."
Joey turns back to the cutting board and says, "It's a wacky world."
Joey Sr. leans closer to Joey.
Joey Sr. asks, "Joe, you ever been in love?"
Joey stops chopping and looks up at his dad.
Joey replies, "I don't know."
Joey Sr. asks, "And you haven't?"
Joey Sr. points toward the stove and says, "You're burning your tomatoes. You'll want to talk?"
Joey quickly walks over to the stove and stirs a pan with a wooden spoon.
Joey Sr. stands upright and says, "Joe, your dad's in love. Big time."
Joey Sr. continues, "And the worst part of it is, it's with two different women." Joey turns to him and says, "Oh, man. Please tell me"
Joey points the wooden spoon at his dad and says, "one of them is Ma."
Joey Sr. throws his hands open and says, "Of course one of them is Ma. What's matter with you?"
Joey lowers the spoon. Joey Sr. points a finger toward Joey's face, then turns away.

In an apartment living room, Joey sits on a sofa holding a peanut butter jar. Rachel stands behind him, massaging his shoulders. Chandler sits on a chair's armrest, Ross sits on an adjacent chair, and Monica sits on the floor nearby.
Rachel continues rubbing Joey's shoulders and says, "It's like if you woke up one day and found out your dad was leading this double life." Joey looks forward.
Rachel says, "He's, like, actually some spy working for the CIA." Joey taps the side of the peanut butter jar.
Joey shakes the jar and moves his hands slightly.
Joey stops moving the jar and says, "That'd be cool."
Joey shifts his gaze slightly.
Joey says, "This blows."
Monica turns her head toward Ross.
Monica asks Ross, "Do you think dad ever cheated on mom?" Ross shakes his head.
Ross says, "Well, no, I don't think so, because that would involve having sex. And I"
Ross continues, "would like to think that our parents don't do that sort of thing," as Chandler and Monica smile slightly.
Rachel walks from behind the sofa and stands beside it, gesturing with one hand.
Rachel, standing near Chandler, crosses her arms and says, "I know. I mean, why can't parents just say parents? Why do they have to become people?"
Rachel continues, "Why do they have," while Chandler's gaze is directed at her torso.
Rachel shifts her posture slightly.
Rachel says, "why" while Chandler continues to look at her torso.
Rachel drops her arms and stares at Chandler.
Rachel says, "can't you stop staring at my breast?" Chandler quickly raises his gaze to her face.
Chandler keeps his gaze on Rachel's face, his mouth slightly open.
Chandler says, "What?"
Rachel places her hands on her hips.
Chandler says, "What," shaking his head slightly.
Rachel shifts her weight.
Rachel asks, "did you not get a good enough look the other day?"
Ross stands up, raises a hand, and says, "All right, we're all adults here."
Ross continues, "There's only one way to resolve this," keeping his arms extended as Chandler and Monica look at him.
Ross gestures toward Chandler and Rachel, saying, "Since you saw her boobies, I think you're going to have to show her your peepee."
Joey extends his hand toward Ross, pointing his finger, while Rachel smiles and points at Ross. Laughter is audible.
Ross sits back down beside Chandler, folding his hands together.
Chandler says, "Don't see that happening," shaking his head.
Rachel turns to Chandler.
Rachel says, "Come on, he's right. Tit for tat," nodding at Chandler.
Chandler looks at Ross, then shifts his gaze. Laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Well, I'm not showing you my tat."
Rachel sits on the sofa next to Joey. The scene changes to the apartment doorway, where Monica walks to an intercom.
Monica presses a button on the intercom and says, "Hello?"
A voice through the intercom says, "It's Phoebe and Roger."
Monica smiles.
Monica speaks into the intercom, saying, "Come on out." Chandler says, "Oh, good."
Chandler continues, "Roger is here."
Joey gestures with one hand toward Chandler.
Joey asks, "What's the matter with Roger?"
Chandler shifts his posture.
Chandler says, "It's nothing. It's a little thing. I hate that guy."
Ross gestures with his hands.
Ross says, "So he was a little analytical. That's what he know," raising his hands slightly.
Ross continues, "Come on, he's not that bad."
The scene changes to a kitchen area where Roger sits at a round table across from Ross and Chandler. Roger points a pen at Ross and says, "You see, that's where you're wrong."
Ross says, "Why would I marry her if I thought on any level that she was a lesbian?"
Roger tilts his head slightly.
Roger says, "I don't know. Maybe you wanted your marriage to fail," lowering his hands.
Ross looks at Roger. Chandler stands up from the table, taking his mug, and walks away.
Ross gestures with both hands and says, "Why would I? Why?"
Roger shifts slightly while Ross keeps his hands raised.
Roger says, "I don't know. Maybe"
Phoebe walks up behind Roger holding two mugs, and Monica stands behind them.
Roger says, "low self esteem," as Phoebe sets a mug in front of him.
Roger says, "Maybe to compensate for overshadowing a sibling."
Monica, stirring a bowl, points her spoon and says, "Maybe you go back to that sibling thing."
Roger raises his hands and says, "Well, I don't know."
Roger lowers his hands.
Roger says, "It's conceivable that you wanted to sabotage your marriage," resting his arm on the table.
Roger continues, "so that the sibling would feel like less of a failure in the eyes of the parents."
Ross's face drops, and he stares forward. Monica looks at Ross.
Ross gestures and says, "That's ridiculous."
Ross gestures toward Monica and says, "I don't feel guilty for her failures."
Monica drops her spoon into the bowl, her eyes wide, and gasps audibly. Laughter is heard.
Monica says, "So you think I'm a failure?"
Roger points at Ross and says, "Isn't he good?" Phoebe replies, "Yeah."
Ross raises his hands and says, "No, that's not what I was saying. You know what I mean."
Monica points her finger at Ross and says, "All these years I thought you were on my side, but maybe what you were"
Monica continues, "doing was sucking up to mom and dad so they'd keep liking you better."
Ross points back at Monica and says, "Hey, I married a lesbian to make you look good," his volume rising slightly on the final words.
Monica stares at Ross with her mouth slightly open. Laughter is audible.
In an apartment living room, Roger sits on the edge of an armchair while Rachel and Phoebe sit on its arms. Rachel wipes her nose with a tissue.
Rachel says, "You're right," pointing her finger.
Rachel lowers her hand slightly.
Rachel adds, "I mean, you're right," her voice shaking as she holds the tissue to her face.
Rachel continues, her voice still breaking, "It wasn't just the Weeble, but it was the Weeble Play Palace" while gesturing with her hands.
Rachel sniffles and rubs her eyes with the tissue. Roger holds one of her hands, and Phoebe rests a hand on her shoulder.
Gesturing with her hands to mimic motion, Rachel says, "and the Weeble Cruise Ship, which had this little lifeboat for the Weebles to wobble in," her voice remaining unsteady.
Rachel wipes a tear from her cheek.
Covering her mouth with her hand, Rachel says, "and mom just gave them all away."
Rachel keeps her hand over her mouth.
Roger pats Rachel's leg and stands up, saying, "That's tough, tough stuff. Come on, Phoebes, we're going to catch that movie. We got to get going," gesturing toward the door.
Phoebe rubs Rachel's back and says, "Okay, feel better," before standing up.
As Phoebe walks past him, Roger says, "Rachel, Phoebes, we're going to be late," touching Phoebe's arm.
Phoebe replies, "Sweet. Okay," continuing to walk.
Phoebe walks behind the sofa toward the kitchen area.
Passing by Monica and Ross sitting at the kitchen table, Phoebe says, "Listen, thanks for everything, mom." Monica, chewing food, replies, "No problem."
Roger puts on his jacket near the door and says, "Listen, guys, it was great seeing you again."
Roger opens the front door.
Standing in the doorway, Roger points at Monica and says, "Mon, easy on those cookies, okay? Remember, they're just food. They're not love."
Roger closes the door. Monica pauses with a cookie halfway to her mouth, then throws it down onto her plate.
Monica says, "I hate that guy." Ross replies, "Mm," and nods. Audience laughter is audible.
The scene changes to a nighttime exterior view of an apartment building, then moves to an interior hallway.
Joey steps out of an apartment door into the hallway and says, "Good night, you guys."
Chandler steps out behind Joey. They look down the hall where a woman in a shiny coat sits on the floor next to a radiator, reading a book.
Chandler says, "Oh, look. It's the woman we ordered."
The woman looks up as Joey and Chandler walk toward her.
Joey asks, "Hey, can we help?" The woman replies, "Ah, no thanks. I'm just waiting for Joey Tribbiani."
Joey places a hand on his chest and smiles, saying, "I'm Joey Tribbiani."
The woman looks at him with wide eyes.
The woman says, "Oh, no, not you. Big Joey. Oh my God, you're so much cuter than your pictures," and stands up.
Joey's smile fades as he glances sideways at Chandler.
The woman places a hand on her chest and says, "I'm Ronnie."
Joey and Chandler look at her.
Ronnie holds out a small plastic bag and says, "Cheese NIB."
Ronnie extends the bag toward Joey, whose face remains still, then she offers it to Chandler.
Chandler takes a piece from the bag and says, "Joey's having an embolism, but I'd go for a NIB."
Chandler eats the snack as Ronnie smiles.
The scene changes to a nighttime view of a city skyline, then cuts to the inside of an apartment. Ronnie sits on a sofa beside Chandler, while Joey stands in the background with his arms crossed.
Ronnie gestures with her hands as she says, "See, most people, when the pets pass on they want them sort of laid out like they're sleeping."
Ronnie lowers her hands.
Moving her hands to demonstrate, Ronnie continues, "But occasionally, you get your person who wants them in a pose. Like chasing their tail"
Ronnie adjusts her posture.
Ronnie raises one hand high and says, "or jumping to catch a Frisbee."
Chandler turns his head toward Ronnie.
Chandler points his thumb over his shoulder toward Joey and says, "Joey, if I go first, I want to be looking for my keys."
Ronnie laughs. Joey Sr. enters the apartment through the front door.
Ronnie says, "That's a good one." Joey Sr. says, "Hey, Joe," and Joey replies, "Hey, dad."
Joey points to the sofa and says, "Ronnie's here."
Joey Sr. stops and looks toward the sofa.
Joey Sr. says, "Hi."
Ronnie stands up and says, "Hey." Joey Sr. smiles and says, "Hello, babe."
Joey Sr. sets his briefcase down on a counter.
Joey Sr. asks, "What are you doing here?"
Ronnie walks toward him.
Holding out a small dark hairpiece, Ronnie says, "Oh, well, you left your good hair at my apartment. I figured you'd need it tomorrow for your meeting."
Joey Sr. takes the hairpiece.
Joey Sr. looks down at the hairpiece and says, "Thank you," his volume softer than his previous line.
Chandler stands up. Joey maintains his crossed arms.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "So who's up for a big game of KerPlunk?"
Joey stares at Joey Sr., who looks away. Ronnie stands silently.
Ronnie says, "Look,"
Ronnie runs a hand through her hair.
Ronnie says, "I shouldn't have come. I better get going. I don't want this last train." Joey Sr. holds his hands out and says, "No, honey."
Joey Sr. continues, "I don't want you taking that thing. This window. Where am I going to stay?" Ronnie replies, "Here."
Joey Sr. gestures with his hands, his mouth moving as he stammers slightly.
Joey maintains a steady gaze on Joey Sr. as the scene continues in silence.
